creator: sheldonh@uunet.co.za
text:
On Mon, 28 Aug 2000 17:50:37 -0400, "J. Duke" wrote:
 
 > On reference at my problem ( PR misc/20811), you demand my dmesg:
 
 Yes, because I was suggesting that your CDROM player software was trying
 to access the wrong device node for your CDROM device.
 
 > OK, this is that dmesg shows:
 
 > acd0: CDROM <IDE/ATAPI CD-ROM 32X> at ata1-master using PIO4
 
 And it looks like I was right. :-)
 
 You don't have a matcd0 device supported by the kernel.  You do have
 an acd0 device.  So read the documentation for your CDROM player
 software and figure out how to get it to read /dev/acd0c instead of
 /dev/rmatcd0c.
 
 :-)
 
 Ciao,
 Sheldon.
